entities.name: FOOD-ENERGY-WATER SYSTEMS
entities.description: Systems integrating food, energy, and water resources that are vulnerable to climate change and natural hazards.

entities.name: NATURAL HAZARDS
entities.description: Environmental events that pose risks to food-energy-water systems.

entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE RISKS
entities.description: Risks resulting from global climate change affecting FEW systems.

entities.name: ADAPTATIONS
entities.description: Actions taken to reduce vulnerabilities in food-energy-water systems.

entities.name: GLOBAL ANALYSIS
entities.description: Large scale assessment of evidence-based FEW-related climate adaptations derived from scientific literature.

entities.name: AFRICA
entities.description: Continent where a high number of FEW-related adaptations were reported.

entities.name: ASIA
entities.description: Continent where a high number of FEW-related adaptations were reported.

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The goal of ensuring reliable access to a sufficient quantity of affordable, nutritious food.

entities.name: WATER SECURITY
entities.description: The goal of ensuring sustainable access to adequate quantities of acceptable quality water.

entities.name: ENERGY SECURITY
entities.description: The goal of ensuring uninterrupted availability of energy sources at an affordable price.

entities.name: GENERALIZED LINEAR REGRESSION MODELS
entities.description: Statistical models used to reveal potential synergies and tradeoffs among FEW adaptations.

entities.name: SYNERGIES
entities.description: Mutually beneficial interactions between different climate adaptations.

entities.name: TRADEOFFS
entities.description: Situations where improving one adaptation may negatively impact another.

entities.name: CONSUMPTION
entities.description: The use of resources within the food-energy-water nexus.

entities.name: PRODUCTION
entities.description: The creation of resources within the food-energy-water nexus.

entities.name: POVERTY
entities.description: Socio-economic condition that creates tradeoffs in FEW adaptations.

entities.name: QUALITATIVE THEMATIC CODING
entities.description: Analysis method used to evaluate the application of the nexus approach in documented adaptations.

entities.name: NEXUS APPROACH
entities.description: An integrated management strategy for food, energy, and water systems to manage scarce resources.

entities.name: SEA LEVEL RISE
entities.description: A climate-driven hazard affecting coastal cities, settlements, and infrastructure.
